title: Model memory payload verification as declarative IR

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the first #1007 slice: externalize memory verify-payload policy into schema-backed package-owned JSON and make existing verification consume it.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not claim verify-payload is portable generated execution yet. Keep command-generation free of memory-specific payload policy. Keep behavior compatible with existing verify_payload tests.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Add the policy schema/artifact, wire installer.verify_payload to read it, update packaging and operation/inventory metadata, regenerate outputs, and validate.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Generated execution or primitive migration requires copying installer policy into command-generation instead of executing declarative policy.
canonical_core.next_action: Close the completed first #1007 slice and continue with the primitive-executor migration later.
proof_expectations: Payload verification policy validates against its schema and mirrors existing constants.
Generated package static proof and Python conformance remain green.
completion_criteria: Memory payload verification policy exists as schema-backed package-owned JSON.
installer.verify_payload consumes the policy for existing verification checks.
#1007 remains open for generated primitive execution of that policy.

validation_commands: uv run pytest packages/memory/tests/test_install.py -k "verify_payload or payload_verification_policy" -q
completion_criteria: Memory payload verification policy exists as schema-backed package-owned JSON.
installer.verify_payload consumes the policy for existing verification checks.
#1007 remains open for generated primitive execution of that policy.

execution_run.what happened: Added payload_verification_policy.schema.json and memory payload_verification.memory.json, wired verify_payload to consume the policy, added schema/constant parity tests, included contracts in the memory package, updated operation/inventory metadata, and regenerated generated command packages.
execution_run.scope touched: Memory contracts, installer verify_payload policy loading, package metadata, generated command snapshots, tests, inventory, and decomposition.
execution_run.changed surfaces: verify-payload truth is now package-owned policy JSON; execution still uses memory.verify-payload.load.
execution_run.validations run: focused memory verify tests, contract tooling, generated package static proof, generated Python conformance, schema reference docs tests, contract tooling tests, and ruff.
execution_run.result for continuation: Continue #1007/#892 with generated primitive execution of payload_verification.memory.json; do not claim verify-payload portable yet.
execution_run.next step: Archive this slice and promote the next bounded #892 slice.

finished_run_review.intent served: first #1007 slice yes; full generated execution remains open

delegation_outcome_feedback.expected savings: Open worker reviews minimal declarative payload verification IR shape while local agent inspects constants and tests.
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: Worker is read-only; implementation will stay bounded to first-slice policy externalization and proof.

proof_report.validation proof: Focused verify_payload tests, contract tooling, generated package checks, Python conformance, schema docs/contract tests, and ruff passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: payload_verification.memory.json validates against payload_verification_policy.schema.json and parity test proves it mirrors current installer constants.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #1007 and #892: represent verify-payload behavior as package-owned declarative truth before moving generated execution to codegen-owned primitives.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The policy truth is declarative and consumed by current runtime, but generated primitive execution remains open.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: memory payload verification policy externalized and consumed
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The #892 decomposition records the remaining generated execution migration.
execution_summary.resume from: select next #892 slice; #1007 next step is generated primitive execution

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: Policy externalization is complete, but verify-payload still executes through package runtime.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: payload_verification.memory.json is the new package-owned truth for existing verify_payload checks.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Policy drift from installer constants or generated verify-payload execution starts without consuming the policy.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
